title: Runtime Sanitizers, Dynamic Analysis, and Language Profiles · Risk Brief: Managed Dynamic
summary: Add runtime instrumentation to native and managed-language test profiles. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates managed dynamic increment in a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use runtime warnings, leak checks, fuzzing, and framework diagnostics for managed languages.
Select thread, memory, leak, race, or platform sanitizers according to risk and compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

steps.title: Defend the Managed Dynamic model
